description: Curated operational risk library for Chinese warehouse automation vendors preparing a first Italian pilot. It identifies evidence buyers will likely ask for and does not certify compliance.

trust_gaps.title: No Italian reference customer
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: The warehouse buyer has no local evidence that the vendor can deploy in Italian operating conditions, procurement culture, and support expectations.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Prepare a bounded first-pilot proposal with measurable KPIs, a clear exit condition, and transferable evidence from similar warehouse workflows.

trust_gaps.title: Unclear local maintenance and spare parts coverage
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: Operations will worry that downtime could block picking, dispatch, or production if support depends only on remote response from China.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Prepare evidence of pilot support hours, escalation contacts, remote diagnostics, spare parts availability in Italy or the EU, and on-site response options.

trust_gaps.title: Incomplete CE/safety evidence
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: The buyer will likely ask for buyer-readable safety evidence before allowing equipment to operate near people, forklifts, goods, or production flows.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Prepare a CE/safety readiness summary, operating limits, emergency-stop behavior, safety features, and a pilot-area risk assessment summary for buyer review.

trust_gaps.title: Unclear WMS integration path
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: IT and operations need to understand whether the pilot can start with a lightweight workflow or requires deeper WMS, ERP, MES, barcode, or task-system integration.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Document the minimum viable integration path, such as operator tablet dispatch, CSV task import, API task handoff, barcode event exchange, and manual fallback.

trust_gaps.title: Non-localized ROI model
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: Finance and operations may reject a generic savings claim if it does not use the buyer's shift pattern, walking-time baseline, throughput baseline, or manual-handling context.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Prepare an ROI model with Italian buyer assumptions and use the pilot to measure the buyer's own baseline before proposing a larger rollout.

trust_gaps.title: Language and documentation readiness
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: Italian supervisors and operators need practical material they can use during onboarding, escalation, daily checks, exceptions, and first-week operation.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Prepare Italian or bilingual quick-start material, operator checklists, supervisor instructions, and escalation notes before the pilot begins.

trust_gaps.title: Installation disruption risk
trust_gaps.buyer_concern: The buyer will likely ask how installation avoids dispatch cutoffs, production stoppages, client SLA disruption, and unsafe mixed-traffic conditions.
trust_gaps.recommended_mitigation: Prepare a bounded installation plan with off-peak work, rollback steps, pilot zone boundaries, safety review, and manual fallback.
